title: podman 1.9.2 fedora32 rootless gating test failures
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Gating tests for rootless are failing and that caused the bodhi update for 1.9.2 to get ejected from the stable set.

See the failures [here](https://jenkins-continuous-infra.apps.ci.centos.org/blue/rest/organizations/jenkins/pipelines/fedora-f32-build-pipeline/runs/484/nodes/220/steps/247/log/?start=0). Thanks @edsantiago .

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. See the Description

**Describe the results you received:**

rootless tests failed


**Describe the results you expected:**

rootless tests should pass

(seems like @giuseppe has WIP for this)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:            1.9.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
lots of:
```
[2020-05-14T04:51:41.506Z]     # error creating temporary file: No such file or directory
[2020-05-14T04:51:41.506Z]     # Error: could not get runtime: error setting up the process: open /run/user/1000/libpod/pause.pid: no such file or directory
[2020-05-14T04:51:41.506Z]     # [ rc=125 ]
```

OK I think I'm getting closer. The [failing bodhi tests](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2020-fb351fcdbb) list three builds, not one, and I think the smoking gun is `conmon`. In September 2019 I added [fedpkg gating tests for conmon](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/conmon/c/92baedb48187e9c18ad63ef957f28fbf9cd6358f?branch=master), and nauseatingly did so by copying podman tests. (There was no better way at the time; still isn't). The rootless-testing mechanism in that copy of the podman tests is broken: it does not do the magic `loginctl` needed by podman. 

I will manually run tests on this conmon build and will report back. This does not seem to be a podman issue.
Tests pass when run manually with the required `loginctl`. I've `+1`ed the bodhi build but it looks like there isn't actually any way to waive test results, despite documentation to the contrary.
